_provenance: Fetched 2026-05-16 from rest.uniprot.org. Human, mouse, rat are reviewed (Swiss-Prot). Chimp is TrEMBL (unreviewed but near-identical to human at protein level — chimp/human SLC22A12 protein identity is >99% as expected for primates).
_use: Used by analyze.py for off-target conservation analysis at the CDS level. A 21-mer siRNA target site that is highly conserved across human + chimp + mouse + rat is reusable for rodent preclinical models AND human therapeutic (siRNAs are mRNA-sequence-specific; cross-species conservation = the same guide strand works in both species). A 21-mer that is human-specific is fine for clinical product but requires species-specific siRNAs for rodent PK/PD.

chimp.note: Unreviewed; included for primate-conservation signal only
